claims.claim: Across 250 random project seeds per condition, we find that delegation yields substantial speedups (up to 2.3×) when coordination overhead is low, but that a naive delegate-all policy becomes counterproductive (0.54×–0.90×) when overhead is high.

evidence_refs.quote: # Delegation Under Coordination Overhead: A Monte Carlo Simulation of Adaptive Task Assignment Thresholds

claims.notes: Historical backfill linked the claim to already published artifacts; this is not independent source revalidation.

claims.claim: An adaptive policy that gates delegation on expected net benefit preserves near-solo performance at high overhead (1.00×–1.04×) while capturing most gains at low overhead (1.95×–2.31×).
